Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The main activities are R Receive A Assign E Evaluate P Approve N Notify C Close The model has a loop between E and P an XOR between N and a skip and a partial order that might allow closing the claim prematurely

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Loop between E and P The loop structure E P suggests that after evaluating you can either exit or approve and loop back to evaluate again This could mean that an evaluation is followed by approval then another evaluation That seems odd Normally youd evaluate once before approving Maybe this loop allows multiple evaluations which isnt standard

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: XOR with N and skip The XOR allows choosing between notifying the customer or skipping it So sometimes the notification might be entirely omitted In a proper process notifying the customer should be mandatory unless theres a specific reason to skip it but the model allows skipping

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Partial orderings allowing premature closure The model has edges RA Aloop loopxor and also AC Since theres no edge from xor to C maybe the system allows C Close to happen right after A bypassing the loop and xor That would mean closing the claim before evaluation or approval Also having AC directly could allow closing without going through the loop and xor steps which is definitely an anomaly

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Business Rule Changes Maybe the loop was added when there was a need to re evaluate claims multiple times like if a claim is rejected and needs to be re evaluated after additional info But if thats not the case the loop might be an error The XOR for skipping notification could be a new rule where sometimes the system auto notifies and sometimes doesnt but maybe the modeler forgot to make it mandatory

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Miscommunication The partial orders might be a result of different teams designing parts of the process without coordination For example the AC edge could be from a team that wanted to allow urgent closures while others wanted to enforce the full process

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Technical Errors Perhaps the workflow tool has limitations where certain constraints cant be enforced leading to these paths being modeled even if they shouldnt be Or maybe the tool allowed adding edges without proper validation leading to loops and skips that shouldnt exist

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Inadequate Controls The modeler might not have had enough constraints to prevent these anomalies For example in POWL strict partial orders might not enforce all necessary dependencies so some edges are missing or added incorrectly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: First looking at the events in claim events The events should follow R A E P N C in order If we see deviations that points to anomalies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Premature Closure Check if there are cases where C occurs before E or P For a claim to be closed there should be an E and P before C

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Multiple Approvals The loop might cause multiple P events So check if any claim has multiple P activities

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Skipped Notification Look for claims where C is followed or preceded by N If N is often missing then the XOR is being used

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait the first query I thought of checks if C is before E or P but maybe also cases where after A theres C but no E P So perhaps a better query would be to see if a claim has A and C but no E or P

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also check for cases where P occurs before E which would be another anomaly But according to the process E should come before P

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Another possibility is if C is happening before N but since the process should be N then C maybe check if C comes before N
